$comment: Blood & Bureaucracy. Pre-production was authored MANUALLY outside the harness (docs at c:/games/project_blood, decisions D-001..D-032). The project is bound here at stage:preproduction so the harness re-runs preproduction-exit as an honest non-trusting gate (rather than trusting the manual self-graded review) after the greybox prototype. See $binding_note.
$binding_note: Bound 2026-07-13 at stage:preproduction with empty stage_history. Upstream concept + concept-greenlight happened in the manual doc phase and are not re-run. The next harness gate to write stage_history is preproduction-exit-wave, which advances to vertical-slice.
name: blood-bureaucracy
description: Campy-gothic indirect-control cult/colony sim: you influence, your followers misinterpret and build a civilization around you.

design.genre: Gothic indirect-control colony sim / exploration RPG
pillars: Influence, never command — every player verb is an act of implication (attention, presence, blessing, relics, petition answers)
Misinterpretation carries weight — follower traits distort intent into action; the intent→outcome gap is the primary story generator, and every event moves a real dial
Growth raises the stakes — success increases Exposure; the late game is harder to keep hidden, not easier to win
Three loops, always open — short (minutes), medium (session), and long (campaign) pulls stay distinctive and non-repetitive
design.art_direction: Campy gothic — spooky world nobody takes too seriously; greybox-first (primitives + flat-color states + readable temp UI) until the core verb is proven fun

narrative.tone: Campy gothic; antagonists are annoyed bureaucrats (Department of Unusual Affairs), not crusading heroes
narrative.delivery: Petitions, misinterpretation outcomes, Rumor Gazette clippings, and enshrined relics; dynamically generated follower names

business.vision: A gothic cult-management survival city builder where your followers build the world you accidentally inspire — 'Build nothing. Influence everything.' The demo anchors a Kickstarter campaign.
business.audience: Colony-sim / indirect-control strategy enthusiasts (Majesty, Against the Storm, RimWorld, Cult of the Lamb players)
business.biggest_risk: The core verb (attention → misinterpretation → settlement growth) fails to land as greybox — or the game slides into the WorldBox trap (autonomous world + passive observer). The prototype-gate fun verdict is the early kill/iterate signal.
